Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Datahäxan 101 upvotes, 8 comments
  2. Dolphin Progress Release 2603 126 upvotes, 12 comments
  3. Printf-Tac-Toe 67 upvotes, 6 comments
  4. WebPKI and You 74 upvotes, 8 comments
  5. Thinnings: Sublist Witnesses and de Bruijn Index Shift Clumping 12 upvotes, 0 comments
  6. Avoiding Trigonometry (2013) 95 upvotes, 19 comments
  7. Show HN: I built a tool that watches webpages and exposes changes as RSS 284 upvotes, 75 comments
  8. 1B identity records exposed in ID verification data leak 121 upvotes, 30 comments
  9. Temporal: The 9-year journey to fix time in JavaScript 734 upvotes, 234 comments
  10. High fidelity font synthesis for CJK languages 15 upvotes, 1 comments
  11. Don't post generated/AI-edited comments. HN is for conversation between humans 3819 upvotes, 1428 comments
  12. Making WebAssembly a first-class language on the Web 610 upvotes, 220 comments
  13. Tested: How Many Times Can a DVD±RW Be Rewritten? Methodology and Results 193 upvotes, 61 comments
  14. 3D-Knitting: The Ultimate Guide 136 upvotes, 45 comments
  15. Show HN: We analyzed 1,573 Claude Code sessions to see how AI agents work 15 upvotes, 2 comments
  16. Show HN: s@: decentralized social networking over static sites 345 upvotes, 153 comments
  17. At Least 17 U.S. Sites Damaged in War with Iran, Analysis Shows 6 upvotes, 0 comments
  18. NASA's DART spacecraft changed an asteroid's orbit around the sun 59 upvotes, 26 comments
  19. Returning to Rails in 2026 225 upvotes, 138 comments
  20. Reliable Software in the LLM Era 37 upvotes, 16 comments
  21. SBCL: A Sanely-Bootstrappable Common Lisp (2008) [pdf] 80 upvotes, 43 comments
  22. Big Data on the Cheapest MacBook 134 upvotes, 81 comments
  23. I was interviewed by an AI bot for a job 368 upvotes, 363 comments
  24. US banks' exposure to private credit hits $300B 7 upvotes, 2 comments
  25. US private credit defaults hit record 9.2% in 2025, Fitch says 91 upvotes, 82 comments
  26. ArcaOS 5.1.2 (based on OS/2 Warp 4.52) now available 5 upvotes, 1 comments
  27. Show HN: Calyx – Ghostty-Based macOS Terminal with Liquid Glass UI 4 upvotes, 1 comments
  28. The MacBook Neo 594 upvotes, 930 comments
  29. Emacs internals: Tagged pointers vs. C++ std:variant and LLVM (Part 3) 3 upvotes, 1 comments
  30. Meticulous (YC S21) is hiring to redefine software dev 0 upvotes, 0 comments

Last generated 10:00pm, 12 March 2026. Source here.
